items.title: VI Live: Excelsior op voorsprong tegen AZ, opmerkelijke rode kaart Bizot
items.summary: Zaterdag staan er vier Eredivisie-duels op het programma. Voor Ajax tegen Fortuna Sittard is er een apart liveblog, voor de rest kun je terecht in deze VI Live. En uiteraard ook voor het belangrijkste voetbal in het buitenland.

items.title: Kane reaches 500 goals - what other records could he break?
items.summary: Bayern Munich striker Harry Kane became the first Englishman to reach 500 career goals on Saturday, but what other records could he break?

items.title: Vink plaatst nuance na ontslag Weijs bij Ajax: 'Zoveel emoties'
items.summary: Marciano Vink heeft het opgenomen voor Willem Weijs, die deze week ontslagen werd als trainer van Jong Ajax. De analist van ESPN vindt het niet terecht dat Weijs afgerekend wordt op de slechte resultaten van het beloftenteam en wijst op de selectiesamenstelling.

items.title: Kompany lacht om jacht Kane op record Lewandowski: 'Ik was geen spits'
items.summary: Harry Kane staat synoniem voor doelpunten. Dat weten ze in Duitsland inmiddels ook al een tijdje. De Engelse topspits slechtte zaterdag de grens van 500 officiële treffers voor club en land door twee keer te scoren tegen Werder Bremen . Daardoor ligt Bayern München nog altijd op titelkoers en ligt er voor Kane wellicht nog een record in het verschiet.

items.title: Aston Villa v Newcastle: FA Cup fourth round – live
items.summary: Updates from 5.45pm (GMT) game at Villa Park Follow us over on Bluesky | And get in touch: email Barry 1 min: Tammy Abraham gets the ball rolling, playing it a few yards backwards to Amadou Onana. Within seconds it finds its way to the feet of Villa goalkeeper Marco Bizot. Not long now: Kieran Trippier and Lucas Digne skipper the sides, which are led out on to the Villa Park pitch by referee Chris Kavanagh and his team of match officials soundtracked by Ozzy Osbourne’s Crazy Train. Kick-off is just a couple of minutes away. Continue reading...

items.title: Bij Marseille is het nooit saai: van keeperswissel tot protest en late tegentreffer
items.summary: Olympique Marseille heeft in een merkwaardig decor in zijn eerste wedstrijd na het ontslag van Roberto De Zerbi een voorsprong door de vingers laten glippen. Protesterende ultras betraden de tribunes pas toen de score werd geopend tegen Strasbourg. De bezoekers kwamen diep in de extra tijd terug van een achterstand.

items.title: Mansfield cause FA Cup upset against Burnley
items.summary: Burnley were on the wrong end of an FA Cup upset as they were beaten 2-1 by Mansfield on Saturday.

items.title: Ronaldo ends protest, scores 18 mins later
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo returned to Al Nassr's lineup for Saturday's match at Al Fateh -- and swiftly scored.

items.title: Man City see off Salford 2-0 in FA Cup 4th round
items.summary: Manchester City reached the fifth round of the FA Cup with a far-from-convincing 2-0 win over League Two side Salford on Saturday.

items.title: Tottenham appoint Igor Tudor as new head coach
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur have appointed Igor Tudor as their new head coach, the club have confirmed.

items.title: Kane scores 500th goal, Bayern cruise past Bremen
items.summary: Harry Kane scored twice to help Bayern Munich to a 3-0 win against Werder Bremen in the Bundesliga on Saturday.

items.title: VI Live: Ajax zonder niet wedstrijdfitte Regeer
items.summary: Ajax neemt het zaterdagavond om 20.00 uur op tegen Fortuna Sittard. De Amsterdammers hopen de draad weer op te pakken na het puntenverlies tegen Excelsior en AZ. Volg de wedstrijd op de voet in de VI Live.

items.title: Skipp scores stunning bicycle kick against Southampton
items.summary: Oliver Skipp scores a stunning overhead kick to bring Leicester City level against Southampton in the fourth round of the FA Cup.

items.title: Southampton and Norwich win all-Championship ties to progress in FA Cup
Mansfield's Reed scores fabulous free-kick to upset Burnley
items.summary: Watch Mansfield Town's Louis Reed produce "set-piece sorcery," scoring a sensational free kick to stun Premier League side Burnley and reach the FA Cup fifth round.

items.title: Guéhi scores first goal for Manchester City in tame FA Cup win against Salford
items.summary: Pep Guardiola spoke of the slog of the schedule and Manchester City performed as if dog-tired when knocking out Salford in a tie the manager pithily described as “boring”. City were abject and half-paced and in danger of being forced into extra time, at least, until Marc Guéhi’s 80th-minute close-range strike doubled the lead. It was the defender’s first goal for the club he joined last month. Continue reading...

items.title: The Jukebox Kid hits right notes as Pauling scores Ascot hat-trick
items.summary: Ben Pauling's fine season continued at Ascot on Saturday, where The Jukebox Kid starred in a treble for the trainer with victory in the Injured Jockeys Fund Ambassadors Programme Reynoldstown Novices' Chase.

items.title: European football: Harry Kane double restores Bayern Munich’s six-point Bundesliga lead
items.summary: England captain has 41 goals in all competitions Quansah on target as Leverkusen thrash St Pauli Harry Kane scored twice in the first half as Bayern Munich cruised to a 3-0 win at Werder Bremen, restoring their six-point lead in the Bundesliga. Borussia Dortmund’s 4-0 win over Mainz on Friday put them within three points of the league leaders but Bayern responded. Bayern were in control from start to finish in Bremen, with Leon Goretzka joining the England captain on the scoresheet in the 70th minute. Kane now has 26 goals in 22 Bundesliga games this season and 41 in all competitions, 13 of those from the penalty spot. Continue reading...

items.title: Guehi scores as Man City beat spirited Salford
items.summary: Marc Guehi scores his first goal for Manchester City to seal a 2-0 win for the Premier League side over League Two Salford City in the fourth round of the FA Cup.

items.title: Burnley turfed out of FA Cup on perfect day for Louis Reed and Mansfield
items.summary: Mansfield gave blood and sweat to reach the FA Cup fifth round for the first time in more than 50 years and reduced a desperate Burnley to tears. A stunning Louis Reed free-kick completed a hard-fought turnaround for the League One side against their labouring Premier League opponents. There is little doubt that Burnley are getting relegated, leaving the Cup as their only hope of salvaging a desperate season, but they lacked quality from start to finish on another dispiriting day for Scott Parker. Mansfield were not necessarily the better side but Nigel Clough’s men worked harder, leaving the fans and players celebrating long after the game was over. Continue reading...
